zh.lifeSummary: 當Joyce de la Zouche於1255年出生在英國戴文金斯 尼普頓時，她的父親William la Zouche47歲，母親Maud Hobridge42歲。她於1261年與Nicholas De Whelton在英國英格蘭戴文金斯 尼普頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1290年3月13日在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年35歲，埋葬在英國武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joyce de la Zouche于1255年出生在英国戴文金斯 尼普頓时，她的父亲William la Zouche47岁，母亲Maud Hobridge42岁。她于1261年与Nicholas De Whelton在英国英格兰戴文金斯 尼普頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1290年3月13日在英国武斯特斯爾伍斯特享年35岁，埋葬在英国武斯特斯爾伍斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Zouche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Zaucha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Franciszek, Kazimierz, Krzysztof, Mieczyslaw, Rafal, Wojciech.
Polish: descriptive nickname from za uchem ‘behind the ear’, denoting someone who had something behind one of his ears. Compare Zoucha .

nameMeanings.name: Joyce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Apparently from the Norman male name Josce (Middle English Josse), which in turn is from Jodocus, a Latinized form of a Breton name, Iodoc, meaning ‘lord’, borne by a 7th-century Breton saint. The name was in use in England among Breton followers of William the Conqueror. However, although this was fairly common as a male given name in the Middle Ages, it had virtually died out by the 14th century. There is evidence of its use as a girl's name from the 16th century onwards in parishes with strong Puritan links, which suggests that it may have been associated with the vocabulary word joy; see Joy . It was strongly revived in the 19th century under the influence of popular fiction. It is borne by characters in Mrs Henry Wood's East Lynne ( 1861 ) and Edna Lyall's In the Golden Days ( 1885 ). Modern use may well have been influenced also by the common Irish surname derived from the medieval Norman male name. See also Joss .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Joyce de la Zouche was born in 1255, in Kings Nympton, Devon, England, her father, William la Zouche, was 47 and her mother, Maud Hobridge, was 42. She married Nicholas De Whelton in 1261, in Kings Nympton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 13 March 1290, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 35, and was buried in Worcester, Worcestershire, England.

ko.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애쉬비 데 라 즈우치
ko.lifeSummary: Joyce de la Zouche(이)가 1255년 영국 데폰 킨그스 니프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William la Zouche(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Hobridge(은)는 42세였다. 그녀는 1261년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 킨그스 니프턴에서 Nicholas De Whelton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1290년 3월 13일 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에 묻혔다.

ru.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joyce de la Zouche родилась в 1255 г. в местности кингс нимптон, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени William la Zouche было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Hobridge, 42. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas De Whelton в 1261 г. в местности кингс нимптон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 13 марта 1290 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 35 лет и была похоронена в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия.

es.of Kings Nympton, Devon, England: Kings Nympton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joyce de la Zouche nació en 1255, en Kings Nympton, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, William la Zouche, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Maud Hobridge, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Nicholas De Whelton en 1261, en Kings Nympton, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 13 de marzo de 1290, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 35 años, y fue sepultada en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra.
